```](#)# Creating your file...This may take a few minutes. Thanks for your patience.CancelYour file is readyOKThe topics in this section show you how to manage cluster health and performance with System Manager in ONTAP 9.7 and later releases.About this taskThis procedure applies to FAS, AFF, and ASA systems. If you have an ASA r2 system (ASA A1K, ASA A90, ASA A70, ASA A50, ASA A30, ASA A20, or ASA C30), follow [these steps](https://docs.netapp.com/us-en/asa-r2/monitor/monitor-performance.html) to monitor your cluster performance. ASA r2 systems provide a simplified ONTAP experience specific to SAN-only customers.You can monitor cluster performance by viewing information about your system on the System Manager Dashboard. The Dashboard displays information about important alerts and notifications, the efficiency and capacity of storage tiers and volumes, the nodes that are available in a cluster, the status of the nodes in an HA pair, the most active applications and objects, and the performance metrics of a cluster or a node.The Dashboard lets you determine the following information:* **Health**: How healthy is the cluster?
* **Capacity**: What capacity is available on the cluster?
* **Performance**: How well is the cluster performing, based on latency, IOPS, and throughput?
* **Network**: How is the network configured with hosts and storage objects, such as ports, interfaces, and storage VMs?In the Health and Capacity overviews, you can click ![Arrow icon](./media/icon_arrow.gif) to view additional information and perform tasks.In the Performance overview, you can view metrics based on the hour, the day, the week, the month, or the year.In the Network overview, the number of each object in the network is displayed (for example, "8 NVMe/FC ports"). You can click on the numbers to view details about each network object.
